sources.label: Investing.com - JEOL Ltd (TYO:6951) price and news
JEOL - Electron Beam Lithography Systems product page (no new order announcements)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already reconciled the adjusted-score rank gap; since June 6 there is no fresh earnings, order, or capacity news and today's tape is flat (-0.21%), so re-moving the name would be churn, not signal.
verdicts.bull: JEOL is a near-duopoly supplier (with NuFlare) of e-beam mask-writing and metrology-grade electron microscopy, a genuine choke point for advanced photomasks, at only ~$2.1B market cap; the last reported quarter beat EPS estimates by roughly 84%.
verdicts.neutral: YTD +29% has already priced in a fair amount of the instrumentation/AI-metrology tailwind, and the medical business transfer to Sysmex (closed April 2026) slims the story without changing the semiconductor thesis; no incremental evidence either way this week.
verdicts.bear: Scientific-instrument demand is tied to academic/government research budgets that are under pressure, and mask-writer orders are lumpy; a pause in advanced-node photomask capex would hit a $2.1B name hard after a 29% YTD run.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New e-beam mask-writer or semiconductor-equipment order disclosures, or the Aug 7, 2026 earnings report confirming margin uplift post-Sysmex divestiture.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order lumpiness and research-budget softness compressing the multiple after the YTD rerating, with no near-term catalyst until August earnings.
